clinical_summary:
Subjective:
The patient presents with worsening abdominal distension and pain. She reports discomfort, confusion, gum bleeding while brushing teeth, and forgetfulness at times. She has a history of Hepatitis C virus (HCV) cirrhosis, HIV on antiretroviral therapy (ART), intravenous drug use (IVDU), Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD), bipolar disorder, Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD), calcification in the breast, skin cancer, dysplasia of the anus, a history of smoking, and a history of cocaine and heroin use. She has no known allergies or adverse drug reactions.

Objective:
Vital signs: Not documented.
Physical examination: Not documented.
Laboratory findings: Not documented.

Assessment:
The patient is a female with a complex medical history, including HCV cirrhosis, HIV on ART, COPD, bipolar disorder, PTSD, skin cancer, dysplasia of the anus, and a history of substance use. She currently reports worsening abdominal distension and pain, along with confusion and forgetfulness. Her past medical history includes self-discontinuation of Lasix and spirnolactone. Current laboratory findings include ALT/AST/AP, TBili, WBC, platelet count, and INR.

Plan:
Will observe the patient for changes in abdominal distension and pain. Will continue to monitor laboratory values, including ALT/AST/AP, TBili, WBC, platelet count, and INR.

SOAP Note:

**Patient Demographics:**
- Age: Not documented
- Gender: Female
- Unit No: Not documented
- Admission Date: Not documented
- Discharge Date: Not documented
- Service: Medicine
- Attending: Not documented

**Chief Complaint:**
Abdominal fullness and discomfort

**History of Present Illness:**
The patient presents with abdominal fullness and discomfort. She has a significant medical history including HCV cirrhosis, COPD, HIV on HAART, ascites, HE, worsening hyponatremia, hyperkalemia, poor appetite, visual hallucinations, forgetfulness, skin cancer, bipolar affective disorder (manic, mild, and PTSD), dysplasia of anus, and a history of cocaine and heroin use. She is currently experiencing abdominal distention and pain, poor appetite, visual hallucinations, and forgetfulness. She denies black/bloody stools, pain or swelling, fevers or chills, chest pain, nausea or vomiting, dysuria or frequency.

**Medications:**
- Ondansetron 4mg IV and morphine 2.5mg IV x1 in the ED

**Allergies:**
- Percocet

**Physical Examination:**
Not documented

**Laboratory Findings:**
Not documented

**Diagnoses:**
- Ascites (Confirmed)
- HE (Confirmed)
- Worsening hyponatremia (Confirmed)
- Hyperkalemia (Confirmed)
- Skin cancer (Confirmed)
- Bipolar affective disorder, currently manic, mild, and PTSD (Confirmed)
- Dysplasia of anus (Confirmed)
- History of cocaine and heroin use (Confirmed)

**Plan:**
Will observe the patient for any changes in symptoms or condition. Continue current medications as prescribed.

SOAP Note:

**Patient Demographics:**

- Gender: Female

**Chief Complaint:**
Altered mental status

**History of Present Illness:**

She has a history of HCV Cirrhosis, HIV, COPD, Bipolar Affective Disorder, PTSD, ascites and hepatic encephalopathy, and a history of cocaine and heroin abuse. She has been experiencing hypotension, lightheadedness, and her memory has started to get fuzzy. She denies abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, chest pain, difficulty breathing, fever, and chills. She has a chronic cough.

Not documented

Percocet, Vicodin

Not documented

- PTSD (Confirmed)

clinical_summary:
Subjective:
The patient presents with a chief complaint of abdominal pain. She reports having pain from her ascites and feels overdue for a paracentesis. She has a worsening girth and reports loose stool a bit more than usual, which she attributes to lactulose use. She denies confusion and is alert and oriented x3.

Objective:
Vital signs not documented. Physical examination not documented.

Assessment:
The patient has a history of HCV Cirrhosis, HIV on HAART, COPD, Bipolar Affective Disorder, PTSD, and a history of cocaine and heroin abuse. She has a history of skin cancer and ascites requiring biweekly therapeutic paracenteses, as well as hepatic encephalopathy. She currently has ascites and hepatic encephalopathy. She has an allergy to Percocet / Vicodin. She is currently on Morphine Sulfate 5 mg IV ONCE MR1 and a GI cocktail.

Plan:
Will observe the patient for any changes in symptoms or condition. Will continue current medications as prescribed.

clinical_summary:
Subjective:
The patient presents with visual hallucinations, worsening gait, falls, gait freezing, confusion, difficulty with ambulation, difficulty standing, and difficulty with simply getting to the bathroom leading to episodes of incontinence. Visual hallucinations include a motor cross race in his backyard. No known allergies or adverse drug reactions are reported.

Objective:
Vital signs not documented. Physical examination not documented.

Assessment:
The patient has a history of dyslipidemia and prostate cancer (status post prostatectomy). Current medications include Mirapex and pravastatin. The patient is experiencing visual hallucinations, gait disturbances, and cognitive symptoms. Cogwheeling of upper extremities and decrease in strength have been ruled out.

Plan:
Will observe the patient for any changes in symptoms. Continue current medications.

clinical_summary:
Subjective:
The patient presents with dysphagia, a worsened foreign body sensation, feeling as though food gets stuck in her neck when she eats, and difficulty breathing when food is stuck in her throat. She has a history of GERD, hypercholesterolemia, kidney stones, mitral valve prolapse, uterine fibroids, osteoporosis, and migraine headaches. She is allergic to omeprazole.

Objective:
Vital signs not documented. Physical examination not documented. Laboratory and imaging results not documented.

Assessment:
The patient is a female with a chief complaint of dysphagia. She has a significant medical history including GERD, hypercholesterolemia, kidney stones, mitral valve prolapse, uterine fibroids, osteoporosis, and migraine headaches. She also has confirmed diagnoses of hiatal hernia, angioectasia in the stomach, and angioectasia in the duodenum. Allergies include omeprazole.

Plan:
Will observe the patient for any changes in symptoms. Will continue to monitor for any signs of complications related to her diagnoses.

clinical_summary:
Subjective:
The patient presents with left hip pain, which is described as immediate and worsens with movement. The patient denies numbness or weakness in the extremities. The patient has a history of GERD, hypercholesterolemia, kidney stones, mitral valve prolapse, uterine fibroids, osteoporosis, and migraine headaches. The patient has allergies to omeprazole, iodine and iodide-containing products, and hallucinogens.

Objective:
Vital signs: Not documented.
Physical examination: Not documented.
Laboratory findings: Not documented.

Assessment:
The patient is a female with a chief complaint of left hip pain. The patient has a significant medical history including GERD, hypercholesterolemia, kidney stones, mitral valve prolapse, uterine fibroids, osteoporosis, and migraine headaches. The patient has known allergies. The patient has a confirmed diagnosis of a left valgus impacted femoral neck fracture.

Plan:
Will observe the patient for any changes in pain or symptoms. Will continue to monitor the patient's condition and vital signs as needed.

clinical_summary:
Subjective:
The patient presents with right flank bruising and pain following a fall. He reports right neck swelling, loss of consciousness, right shoulder hematoma, pain in his right shoulder, and bruising. He has a past medical history of factor VIII deficiency. He has no known allergies or adverse drug reactions.

Objective:
Vital signs: Not documented.
Physical examination: Not documented.
Laboratory findings: Not documented.
Diagnostic studies: Not documented.

Assessment:
The patient is a 46-year-old male with a chief complaint of right flank bruising and pain secondary to a fall. He has a past medical history of factor VIII deficiency. His current symptoms include right neck swelling, loss of consciousness, right shoulder hematoma, pain in his right shoulder, and bruising. His diagnoses include no intracranial hemorrhage, thickened right platysma muscle with surrounding hematoma, active contrast extravasation within the right platysma muscle, right shoulder hematoma, muscular hemorrhage along the flank, and hemophilia A.

Plan:
Will observe the patient for any changes in symptoms or condition. Continue current medications including DDAVP IV, factor VIII assay, vW level, DDAVP intranasally, chemistries and coags, FVIII activity, CTAP, and desmopressin 0.3mg/kg IV.

clinical_summary:
Subjective:
The patient presents with a chief complaint of epistaxis. He reports persistent nosebleed, nasal fractures, some trauma to his face, a small abrasion to the bridge of his nose, nausea, coughing up blood, lightheadedness, hypotension, bradycardia, and a brief loss of consciousness. He has a history of AAA repair, myocardial infarction after AAA repair, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia. He has no known allergies or adverse drug reactions.

Objective:
Vital signs: Not documented.
Physical examination: Not documented.
Laboratory findings: Not documented.
Diagnostic studies: CT scan of the head, neck, and face revealed a nasal bone and septal fracture. Chest X-ray demonstrated a focal consolidation at the left lung base.

Assessment:
The patient is suffering from epistaxis, nasal bone and septal fracture, and a focal consolidation at the left lung base. He has a past medical history of AAA repair, myocardial infarction after AAA repair, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia.

Plan:
The patient will be observed for any changes in his condition. He will continue to receive amoxicillin-clavulanate for antibiotic prophylaxis, ondansetron, Normal Saline (NS), and metoprolol tartrate.
